name: VERSATILE VEGETABLE soup
intro: When I’m lazy and want to eat light, I make this vegetable-laden soup. I serve it with rice, a quick protein—which may or may not involve meat—and if available, pickled daikon and carrot (see this page). Vegetables are the stars here, so there’s no need to prepare a separate vegetable side dish.

ingredients.item: medium yellow or red onion, thinly sliced
medium carrot, or 1 small sweet potato, cut into thumbnail-size chunks
bean sprouts or ribbons of pinkie-length napa or green cabbage leaves
fresh shiitake or white mushrooms, stems included, thickly sliced
fish sauce, or 1½ teaspoons soy sauce, Bragg Liquid Aminos, or Maggi Seasoning sauce, plus more as needed
thinly sliced green onion, white part only, or 1 garlic clove, minced
coarsely chopped fresh herbs, such as cilantro, dill, or basil, or any combination
Recently ground black pepper, chile garlic sauce (see this page), or sambal oelek for garnish

method: In a 3- to 4-quart saucepan over medium heat, warm the canola oil. When the oil is barely shimmering, add the onion and ginger and cook, stirring frequently, for about 4 minutes, until the onion softens. Add the carrot, celery, bean sprouts, and mushrooms; toss in ¾ teaspoon salt and the fish sauce; and cook, stirring, for 1 to 2 minutes to develop flavor and warm up. Pour in the water, partially cover, and turn the heat to high. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and cook, uncovered, at a vigorous simmer for 15 minutes. Increase the heat to return to a boil, and add the kale. When just cooked through, about 1 minute, turn off the heat and stir in the green onion.
Let the soup rest, uncovered, for 5 minutes, then taste and, if needed, add more salt or fish sauce. (Splash in water if you’ve gone too far or if the soup isn’t brothy enough for you.)
Ladle the soup into individual bowls and divide the herbs among them. Sprinkle on some pepper or plop in a little chile sauce for lively heat, and serve.
